title: tephrosia gorgonea
intro: Tephrosia gorgonea, also known as the snake vine or wild calabar bean, is a perennial climber native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Tephrosia gorgonea include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a range of ailments, from wounds and infections to gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia gorgonea can be made by drying and processing the leaves and stems, which can be used in teas, infusions, or as a topical application.
